title: 10 best herbal teas for frozen shoulder

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains Salvia miltiorrhiza, a plant-based compound that reduces inflammation and promotes blood flow. As frozen shoulder is characterized by stiffness, pain, and limited mobility due to reduced blood supply and inflammation, the anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties of danshen tea may help alleviate these symptoms. By improving circulation and reducing swelling, danshen tea may also aid in the healing process, allowing for greater range of motion and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Danshen tea's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
Analgesic property: The analgesic property of Danshen tea can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by frozen shoulder, allowing patients to manage their symptoms more effectively.

Blood circulation promoting property: By improving blood circulation, Danshen tea may help to reduce stiffness and improve range of motion in the affected shoulder joint, reducing the severity of frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, danshen roots are used to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them effective in treating frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Containing saponins and other active constituents, rhyzomes of danshen plant help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried danshen root in quantities of 30g per serving.
Boil 500ml water and steep the danshen root for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 5-10g of dried ginger slices to enhance digestion and reduce nausea.
Allow the tea to cool before drinking within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet annie teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols also improve blood circulation, which increases oxygen and nutrient supply to the muscles and tendons, promoting healing and relaxation. Additionally, sweet annie's sedative properties help alleviate stress and anxiety, common underlying causes of frozen shoulder. By addressing these factors, sweet annie teas can provide relief from stiffness, pain, and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet Annie tea contains artemisinin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, which can be beneficial in reducing stiffness and discomfort associated with Frozen Shoulder.
Analgesic: The tea's analgesic properties can help relieve pain and discomfort in the affected shoulder joint, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.

Antioxidant: Sweet Annie tea's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, which may aid in reducing scar tissue formation and improving range of motion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with Frozen Shoulder.
Barks: The barks contain alkaloids that can help relax muscles and improve joint mobility, making them effective in treating the stiffness and rigidity of Frozen Shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried artemisia annua leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the herb in the hot water for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids after the steeping time has elapsed.
Add one tablespoon of honey or other sweetener to taste and stir well.
Drink the tea three times a day as needed or directed by a healthcare provider.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains a unique blend of herbs that promote blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and relax tense muscles. The tea's warming properties help to loosen stiff joints, allowing for greater mobility and flexibility. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in Yanhusuo teas may help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it an effective natural remedy for relief and recovery. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: Yanhusuo tea has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area, which is essential for relieving the stiffness and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Muscle relaxant properties: The active compounds in Yanhusuo tea can help relax tense muscles, improve joint mobility, and reduce muscle spasms, making it an effective treatment for frozen shoulder symptoms like limited range of motion and pain with movement.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: Yanhusuo tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the affected joints from oxidative damage caused by inflammation, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation.

Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes and alkaloids that help to relieve stiffness and promote relaxation of the affected muscles.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried corydalis yanhusuo root and 1 teaspoon of dried angelica dahurica root into a small bowl.
Boil 2 cups of pure water in a teapot for 5 minutes before reducing heat to simmer.
Steep the herbal mixture in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to infuse and combine.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup, discarding any solids and leaving only the liquid extract.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming, sipping on it warm or at room temperature throughout the day as needed for pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai teas helps with frozen shoulder because of its ability to promote blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and relieve pain. remedy_recipe: Gather dried angelica sinensis roots and measure out 2 teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a medium-sized pot then reduce heat to simmer.
Add the 2 teaspoons of dried dong quai roots to the simmering water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a large mug and add honey or lemon to taste if desired after cooling slightly.
Allow the tea to cool completely before refrigerating it for up to 3 days or freezing it for later use.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric powder and 1 cup of boiling water to create the tea mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with frozen shoulder because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and eases pain. remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried licorice root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Heat one cup of water in a medium-sized pot over low heat for 5 minutes, allowing it to reach a gentle simmer.
Add the ground licorice powder to the hot water, stirring continuously for 1 minute to prevent lumps from forming.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties of the licorice root to infuse into the tea for optimal benefits.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving hot as needed, up to three times daily for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of Wild Marjoram can help alleviate the chronic pain associated with Frozen Shoulder, providing relief from constant ache and discomfort.

Antispasmodic: Wild Marjoram's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms, which is particularly helpful in reducing the stiffness and limited range of motion often experienced with Frozen Shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation and relax muscles.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which aid in relieving pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried origanum vulgare leaves from a reliable source or store-bought package for future use.
Measure one teaspoon of the dried herb and pour it into a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Boil fresh filtered water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the herb, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before consuming within the next 30 minutes, taking one 1/2 cup serving three times daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frozen shoulder with herbal teas is to consume warm beverages containing turmeric, ginger, and willow bark. Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties reduce pain and swelling, while ginger's warming effects increase blood flow to the affected area. Willow bark's salicylic acid helps alleviate stiffness and discomfort. Drink 2-3 cups daily for several weeks to experience relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal teas are arthritis, tendonitis, and bursitis. Herbal teas such as turmeric, ginger, and devil's claw have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling. Teas made from willow bark, meadowsweet, and valerian root can also provide relief from muscle spasms and stiffness associated with these conditions. Drinking these herbal teas regularly may help alleviate symptoms and promote overall joint health.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for frozen shoulder are natural remedies that have been used to alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with this condition. They help by reducing inflammation, relieving muscle spasms, and promoting blood flow to the affected area. Examples of herbal teas that help with frozen shoulder include turmeric tea, ginger tea, and willow bark tea. By consuming these teas, individuals with frozen shoulder can improve their range of motion, reduce pain, and enhance their overall quality of life.
